rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MIKHAIL M. VAYNBERG AND BEATRISA VAYNBERG LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Remove Trustee, For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty, And For Partition
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. This petition appears premature for the court's review at this time. The examiner will review further when the above issues are cleared. (1) The petition lists only Yafim and the petitioner as entitled to notice, omitting both the Respondent and the sureties. Proper Notice of Hearing and Notice of Remote Instruction must be provided to all individuals entitled to notice, and the petition should be supplemented to include these persons. (2) No proposed order has been submitted. (3) The petition must specify the legal authority under which the Court may grant partition. Although the caption and prayer request partition, the body of the petition does not sufficiently address this relief. (4) Additionally, the petition should propose a successor trustee for the Court's consideration, along with the nominee's consent to serve. (5) Petition does not report regarding the petitioner's request for the account made to the trustee. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE MARY L. MCFADDEN SURVIVOR'S TRUST, ESTABLISHED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (6096 & 6075 Austin Creek Rd. Cazadero, Ca 95421)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues to be addressed at hearing: 1) The petition appears to be premature. Petitioner did not publish notice of sale (LRSF 14.31), mail notice of hearing to the interested parties or the buyers, and the appraisal was not completed by a probate referee or filed on the mandatory Judicial Council Form (LRSF 14.32.A). 2) Commission is 6%. The Court will ordinarily allow brokers' commissions in a total amount not to exceed 5% of the sale price (LRSF 14.33.A). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although (1)no courtesy copies were provided, as required by LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B., (2) the decedent's date of death, according to the death certificate attached to the initial Petition for Letters of Administration, was 12/14/16, not 12/4/16, and, (3) had notice been required, a printout of the instructions for how to appear remotely would have been required to be attached to the Notice of Hearing. No appearance will be required on that date if a petition is filed at least two weeks before that date.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERVASIO TOMAS GAYOSO DE OCAMPO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/7/26. (No earlier availability.) Although no courtesy copies were received by the assigned examiner by the 1/9/26 deadline, the papers were reviewed online in hopes of not continuing the hearing. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] (1) Has Wendy L. Smith consented to serve? (Who represents her?) (2) How does Petitioner have standing? Only Ramon G. De Ocampo is a resident of the United States. [Probate Code Section 8402(a)(4).] (No declination to serve, signed by Maria Concepcion O. Pasion, appears to have been filed, although she also does not appear to be a person entitled to Letters.) (3) The decedent's parents' dates of death were not set forth. (4) Notice must have attached to it a PRINTOUT of the procedures for how to make a remote appearance, not merely a link to the Court's website. Courtesy copies of the any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 3/24/26 to avoid another continuance. (A new proof of service or proposed order, etc., should just be filed or submitted, as the case may be, not attached to a supplement.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STELLA YING NORMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/1/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The petition caption appears to be incomplete, as does item 2c of the petition, when the proposed order indicates that full IAEA authority is to be granted. (2) The Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties & Liabilities is incomplete (no phone number). (3) Item 7 of the petition must be completed if Decedent was not survived by spouse or issue. (4) Who is/was Decedent's father? If he is deceased, when did he die? [LRSF 14.18 C. and D.] (5) An ex-spouse is not an heir. Why is notice being given to him in care of attorneys, even though he is also reportedly deceased? (6) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] Please provide courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/20/26. A supplement, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, must be filed to address issues with the petition. A new, complete Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities must be filed. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCOISE EMILIE GRANDI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing to admit the will. No appearance required. Status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for 07/20/2027.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARIO DEBONO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing solely to admit the will. No appearance required. Status hearing: 7/20/2027. The personal representative shall file either a petition for an order for final distribution of the estate or a report of the status of administration in compliance with Prob. Code sec. 12200 et seq. two weeks before the hearing date.
